subject: Ecology
title: Distance and Immigration
description: Learn Distance and Immigration in one minute.

hook.text: The farther an island sits from the mainland, the fewer species manage to reach it.

buildup.text: Every kilometer of open water is a barrier that filters out weaker dispersers.

discovery.text: Only strong fliers, wind-carried seeds, and lucky rafters colonize remote oceanic islands.

twist.text: Hawaii, 3,800 km from the nearest continent, was colonized by only about 750 founding species.

climax.text: Those few founders then radiated into thousands of unique species found nowhere else.

punchline.text: Distance is a filter. It decides who arrives.

quiz.question: Why does distance reduce island species richness?
options: Fewer species can cross wide barriers
